content.problemStatement:
Ben is fighting a monster with a health of HHH. He starts with an attack power of 111.

Ben has two types of moves:


He can use a regular attack, which damages the monster by his current attack power.

After this, his attack power **doubles**.
He can use a special move: choose a **prime number** PPP such that P≤HP \leq HP≤H (HHH being the current health of the monster), and deal PPP damage to the monster.

This move can be done **at most once**.

Note that this special move doesn't affect his attack power: it doesn't double, and remains the same.
To kill the monster, Ben must deal **exactly** HHH damage to it.

Find the minimum number of moves needed for Ben to kill the monster, or print −1-1−1 if it's impossible to kill it no matter what.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains one integer HHH — the initial health of monster.


Output Format

For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of moves Ben will perform to kill the monster (if he's able to do so at all).

If he is unable to kill the monster no matter what, output −1-1−1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Ben can do the following:

Initially, the monster has H=10H = 10H=10 health.
Use a regular attack, with power 111. The monster's health drops to 999, and the regular attack's power increases to 222.
Use a special attack with P=7P = 7P=7. HHH becomes 222.
Use a regular attack, with power 222. The monster is defeated.

Test case 222: It can be shown that no matter what sequence of moves is chosen, Ben cannot deal exactly 212121 damage. So, the answer is −1-1−1.
Test case 333: There exists a sequence of 151515 moves that kills the monster, and it can be shown that achieving this in 141414 or less moves is impossible.
